title: My Favourite Food
summary: A woman talks about her favourite food — pasta. She describes how she makes a simple tomato pasta at home and why she loves it so much.
body: My favourite food is pasta. I eat pasta at least twice a week. There are many types of pasta, but I like spaghetti the most. It is long and thin. I usually make a simple tomato sauce at home. First, I heat some oil in a pan. Then I add garlic and onion. I cook them for a few minutes. After that, I add a tin of tomatoes. I stir everything together and add a little salt and pepper. I let it cook for about fifteen minutes. While the sauce is cooking, I boil the spaghetti in hot water. When everything is ready, I mix the pasta with the sauce. I put it in a big bowl. I like to add some cheese on top. The smell is wonderful. The taste is rich and warm. It is a perfect meal for a cold evening. I always feel happy when I eat pasta. It reminds me of cooking with my grandmother when I was young.

annotations.meaningVi: ít nhất hai lần một tuần
annotations.exampleEn: She goes to the gym at least twice a week.
He made spaghetti with a meat sauce for dinner.
Heat some oil in the pan before adding the vegetables.
Add two cloves of garlic to the sauce for extra flavour.
Open a tin of tomatoes and add it to the pan.
Stir the soup so it does not stick to the bottom of the pan.
Season the dish with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Boil the pasta in salted water for ten minutes.
The soup had a rich and warm flavour on a cold day.
This song reminds me of my childhood.
When I was young, I lived in a small village.

grammarNotes.title: Sequencing instructions with First, Then, After that
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả các bước nấu ăn hoặc hướng dẫn, dùng các từ nối tuần tự: 'First' (đầu tiên), 'Then' (sau đó), 'After that' (tiếp theo). Ví dụ: 'First, I heat some oil. Then I add garlic and onion. After that, I add a tin of tomatoes.' Những từ này giúp người nghe hoặc người đọc hiểu đúng thứ tự các bước cần thực hiện.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: When describing steps in a recipe or instructions, use sequence words: 'First', 'Then', 'After that'. Example: 'First, I heat some oil. Then I add garlic and onion. After that, I add a tin of tomatoes.' These words help the listener follow the correct order of actions.

grammarNotes.title: While – Two actions happening at the same time
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'while' để nói về hai hành động xảy ra đồng thời: 'While the sauce is cooking, I boil the spaghetti.' Nghĩa là: Trong khi sốt đang nấu, tôi luộc spaghetti. Cả hai việc xảy ra cùng lúc. So sánh với 'when' chỉ dùng cho một điểm thời gian cụ thể: 'When everything is ready, I mix the pasta.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'While' is used to show that two actions happen at the same time: 'While the sauce is cooking, I boil the spaghetti.' Both actions are happening simultaneously. Compare this with 'when', which refers to a specific point in time: 'When everything is ready, I mix the pasta.'

translationVi: Món ăn yêu thích của tôi là mì ống. Tôi ăn mì ống ít nhất hai lần một tuần. Có nhiều loại mì ống, nhưng tôi thích spaghetti nhất. Nó dài và mỏng. Tôi thường tự làm sốt cà chua đơn giản ở nhà. Đầu tiên, tôi đun nóng một ít dầu trong chảo. Sau đó tôi cho tỏi và hành vào. Tôi nấu chúng vài phút. Tiếp theo, tôi cho một hộp cà chua vào. Tôi khuấy mọi thứ với nhau và cho một chút muối và tiêu. Tôi để nó nấu khoảng mười lăm phút. Trong khi sốt đang nấu, tôi luộc spaghetti trong nước sôi. Khi mọi thứ đã xong, tôi trộn mì với sốt. Tôi cho vào một cái bát to. Tôi thích cho thêm một chút phô mai lên trên. Mùi thơm thật tuyệt vời. Vị đậm đà và ấm áp. Đây là bữa ăn hoàn hảo cho một buổi tối lạnh. Tôi luôn cảm thấy vui khi ăn mì ống. Nó gợi nhớ cho tôi về những lần nấu ăn cùng bà khi tôi còn nhỏ.

questions.prompt: What is the narrator's favourite food?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'My favourite food is pasta.'
questions.hint: What does the narrator say at the very start?

questions.prompt: How often does she eat pasta?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I eat pasta at least twice a week.'
questions.hint: How many times per week does she eat it?

questions.prompt: Which type of pasta does she like the most?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I like spaghetti the most.'
questions.hint: Which long, thin pasta does she prefer?

questions.prompt: What does she add to the hot oil first?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I add garlic and onion.'
questions.hint: What goes into the pan after the oil?

questions.prompt: What does she add after the garlic and onion?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'After that, I add a tin of tomatoes.'
questions.hint: What is the key ingredient in a tomato sauce?

questions.prompt: How long does she let the sauce cook?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I let it cook for about fifteen minutes.'
questions.hint: How many minutes does the sauce need?

questions.prompt: What does she do while the sauce is cooking?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'While the sauce is cooking, I boil the spaghetti.'
questions.hint: What does she do at the same time as the sauce cooks?

questions.prompt: What does she put on top of the pasta?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I like to add some cheese on top.'
questions.hint: What topping does she add to the finished dish?

questions.prompt: When is pasta a perfect meal, according to the narrator?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'It is a perfect meal for a cold evening.'
questions.hint: What kind of weather or time does she mention?

questions.prompt: What does eating pasta remind the narrator of?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'It reminds me of cooking with my grandmother when I was young.'
questions.hint: Who was she cooking with in her memory?

questions.prompt: How does the narrator describe the taste of her pasta?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'The taste is rich and warm.'
questions.hint: What two adjectives describe the flavour?

I usually make a simple tomato {{b3}} at home. First, I heat some oil in a pan. Then I add {{b4}} and onion. I cook them for a few minutes. After that, I add a tin of tomatoes. I {{b5}} everything together and add a little salt and pepper. I let it cook for about fifteen {{b6}}. While the sauce is cooking, I {{b7}} the spaghetti in hot water. When everything is ready, I mix the pasta with the sauce. I put it in a big bowl. I like to add some {{b8}} on top. The smell is wonderful. The taste is rich and {{b9}}. It is a perfect meal for a cold evening. I always feel happy when I eat pasta. It reminds me of cooking with my {{b10}} when I was young.

blanks.explanation: 'Pasta' — the narrator's favourite food and the subject of the whole passage.
'Spaghetti' — the type of pasta she likes most; long and thin.
'Tomato sauce' — the sauce she makes at home for her pasta.
'Garlic and onion' — the first ingredients added to the hot oil.
'Stir everything together' — mixing the ingredients in the pan.
'Fifteen minutes' — the cooking time for the tomato sauce.
'Boil the spaghetti' — cooking pasta in hot water is called boiling.
'Some cheese on top' — a classic pasta topping.
'Rich and warm' — the comforting taste she describes.
'My grandmother' — the person she used to cook with as a child.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says that eating pasta reminds her of cooking with her grandmother. Why do you think certain foods can bring back strong memories? Do you have a food that reminds you of a special person or place?
